name: RainRoute
description: RainRoute is a weather-driven navigation app that helps people plan everyday trips around more than just distance and traffic. It combines hyperlocal precipitation forecasts, wind, temperature swings, shade, shelter access, and sidewalk conditions to recommend the most comfortable path for walking, biking, transit transfers, and short deliveries. The app solves the common problem of getting caught off guard by weather on routine trips, like arriving soaked after taking the "shortest" route through an exposed block or missing a train because a sudden downpour slowed everything down. It helps users in daily life by adjusting routes in real time, suggesting when to leave earlier, where to wait under cover, and which nearby stop or entrance is least exposed. For example, a commuter can choose the route with the fewest open intersections during gusty rain, a parent can find the best covered path to school, and a cyclist can avoid streets with unsafe crosswind conditions. What makes RainRoute unique is that it treats weather as part of the route itself, not just a forecast notification, turning predictions into practical movement decisions.
features: The app calculates weather-optimized routes by comparing exposure to rain, wind, heat, and cold, so users can choose the path that feels best rather than simply the fastest one.
It sends minute-by-minute departure recommendations based on incoming weather shifts, helping users leave early enough to avoid getting trapped in a storm or heat spike.
It highlights sheltered waiting spots, covered walkways, and indoor shortcuts near the user’s route, which matters when a transfer or errand requires time outdoors.
It flags risky micro-conditions like strong crosswinds on bridges or slippery stretches during freezing drizzle, giving users practical safety guidance instead of generic alerts.
It learns from repeated trips and personal preferences, such as whether the user values dryness, speed, or shade, so the routes become more useful over time.

audience': 'Urban commuters, cyclists, parents doing school drop-offs, delivery riders, and active adults aged roughly 18–55 who regularly move through weather-exposed cities and suburbs. It is especially useful for people in places with unpredictable rain, extreme heat, or windy conditions where short trips can quickly become uncomfortable.
